arguments.task: Research Twenty CRM using only public pages. Visit official Twenty website and official documentation or developer pages. Find and extract: a one-line product description; whether there is a public API and its surface type (REST, GraphQL, both, or none); authentication methods documented for the API; pricing or access gating relevant to API use; rate limit notes; and whether an official or existing MCP server/integration page exists (official docs, GitHub, or MCP registry). Prefer first-party docs, pricing, support, changelog, and GitHub pages. Return a concise evidence-backed summary with exact source URLs for each finding. Do not use blog posts unless first-party and necessary.

input.thought: Start browser research on official Twenty sources and supporting registry/GitHub pages.

Product Description**\\n*   **Description:** Twenty gives technical teams the building blocks for a custom CRM that meets complex business needs and quickly adapts as the business evolves.\\n*   **Source:** [twenty.com](https://twenty.com)\\n\\n**2. API Surface Type**\\n*   **Types:** Both **REST** and **GraphQL** APIs are available.\\n*   **Source:** [Twenty Developer Documentation - APIs](https://docs.twenty.com/developers/extend/api)\\n\\n**3. Authentication Methods**\\n*   **API Key:** Standard authentication via a 'Personal Access Token' passed as a Bearer token in the authorization header.\\n*   **OAuth:** Supported for external applications to access workspace data.\\n*   **Source:** [Twenty Developer Documentation - APIs](https://docs.twenty.com/developers/extend/api)\\n\\n**4. Pricing & API Gating**\\n*   **Access:** API access is included in both the **Pro** ($9/user/month) and **Organization** ($19/user/month) plans.\\n*   **Gating:** Advanced features like SSO and Row-level permissions are gated to the Organization plan, but the core APIs (REST/GraphQL) and MCP server are available on both.\\n*   **Source:** [twenty.com/pricing](https:/...[truncated]

raw_result.description: Open-source CRM for technical teams with customizable data model and workflow building blocks.
raw_result.auth_notes: Twenty documents Personal Access Tokens used as Bearer tokens for individual/server access, and OAuth for external applications accessing workspace data.
raw_result.gating_reason: Public pricing page indicates API access and MCP server are included on paid Pro and Organization plans; advanced developer-related limits/features scale by plan.

api_surface.breadth: Public developer docs state both REST and GraphQL APIs are available for extending Twenty and accessing workspace data.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Twenty publicly lists an MCP server as a Developers feature on its pricing page, indicating first-party MCP support, but the exact repository/docs page for the MCP server was not verified in available evidence.

raw_result.blocker: API appears buildable from public docs, but self-serve free access to hosted API was not verified; pricing evidence points to paid plans for API access/MCP, so initial toolkit development likely requires a paid plan or self-hosting confirmation.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Developer docs mention a default limit of 100 requests per minute for API keys; pricing page shows plan-based API call limits of 50/min for Pro and 100/min for Organization, creating some ambiguity that should be checked during implementation.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official public developer documentation exists; API supports both REST and GraphQL; auth supports PAT/Bearer and OAuth; pricing page explicitly references an MCP server and developer feature set; Twenty is positioned as open-source/customizable CRM.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Pricing vs docs create ambiguity on exact API availability and rate limits across free/self-hosted vs paid cloud plans, and MCP existence is only confirmed from pricing language rather than a directly verified MCP documentation or repository page.

result.gating_reason: Public pricing page indicates API access and MCP server are included on paid Pro and Organization plans; advanced developer-related limits/features scale by plan.

result.notable_signals: Official public developer documentation exists; API supports both REST and GraphQL; auth supports PAT/Bearer and OAuth; pricing page explicitly references an MCP server and developer feature set; Twenty is positioned as open-source/customizable CRM.
result.human_review_reason: Pricing vs docs create ambiguity on exact API availability and rate limits across free/self-hosted vs paid cloud plans, and MCP existence is only confirmed from pricing language rather than a directly verified MCP documentation or repository page.
